name: Medical Office Cleaning
criticalZone: exam tables, blood pressure cuffs, door hardware, waiting-room touch points
centralRisk: patient-to-patient transfer via high-touch surfaces between visits
regulatoryFramework: CDC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control in Health-Care Facilities
105 CMR 140 — Massachusetts DPH, Licensure of Clinics

commonSurveyFindings.finding: No written schedule for cleaning and method of decontamination based on location, surface type, soil type and the procedures performed. This is an explicit OSHA requirement, not a recommendation.
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: Federal OSHA cited 29 CFR 1910.1030 thirty-three times across twelve inspections of physicians' offices between October 2024 and September 2025 — the most-cited standard for the sector, out of 55 citations in total.

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Contaminated work surfaces not decontaminated after procedures, immediately after a spill, and at the end of a shift where the surface may have become contaminated.
Protective coverings on equipment and surfaces not removed and replaced when contaminated or at the end of the shift.
Reusable bins, pails and cans likely to become contaminated are not inspected and decontaminated on a schedule, and not cleaned immediately when visibly contaminated.
Broken contaminated glassware picked up by hand instead of by mechanical means such as a brush and dustpan, tongs or forceps.
Environmental surfaces in exam rooms, especially those near the patient and frequently touched, not cleaned and then disinfected with an EPA-registered disinfectant.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CDC Infection Prevention Checklist for Outpatient Settings, Section II item A; CDC Core Practices 5b

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Staff cleaning the practice have not been trained on, or issued, the PPE needed to avoid exposure to infectious agents and to the disinfectant chemicals themselves.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CDC Infection Prevention Checklist for Outpatient Settings, Section I item C

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Filled sharps containers not disposed of under state regulated medical waste rules, and regulated waste containers not closable, leak-proof and labelled or colour-coded.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CDC Infection Prevention Checklist, Section II items I and J; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1030(d)(4)(iii)

_publishGate: Requires >=10 faqs and >=6 commonSurveyFindings before this service may go live. Survey findings are real regulatory citations — never invented, never inferred from the regulation text. Source: data/_todo-clinical-input.md.
